title: PSLE 2016 Science (Booklet B)
subtitle: Ministry of Education, Singapore — Primary School Leaving Examination 2016 — Science (0009/02 B).  Part II consists of 14 open-ended questions, Q31–Q44 (40 marks).  Total time for Booklets A and B: 1 hour 45 minutes.  For questions 31 to 44, write your answers in this booklet.  The number of marks available is shown in brackets [ ] at the end of each question or part question.  Diagrams have been left blank — click each paste-zone and Ctrl/⌘+V the corresponding image from the original paper.  Open-ended boxes have the same number of writing lines as the answer spaces in the original paper.

elements.text: Part II.  For questions 31 to 44, write your answers in this booklet.  The number of marks available is shown in brackets [ ] at the end of each question or part question.  (40 marks)
Question 31.  An animal M grows on the shell of a crab as shown below.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Animal M has a poisonous sting.  Explain how the crab would benefit from its relationship with animal M.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Animal M can also grow on a rock.  Explain why it is an advantage for animal M to grow on the crab instead of growing on a rock.  [1]

Question 32.  To store seeds for long periods of time, a farmer placed them in a large underground hole as shown.

The hole was filled completely with seeds and the opening was sealed to prevent rainwater and air from entering.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: After some time, the farmer found that seeds in the outer layer of the hole germinated but the seeds at the centre of the hole did not germinate.  Explain why.  [2]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Would the seeds at the centre of the hole decompose after some time?  Explain your answer.  [1]
Question 33.  At the beginning, island Q had plants and animals but island R had no plants and no animals.
A few years later, island R started to have plants.  Later on, animals were also found there.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Suggest one way how island R started to have plants.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Give a reason why animals could be found living on island R only after plants were found growing there.  [1]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text:
After some time, a scientist observed that the population of animals on island Q remained constant but the population of animals on island R increased rapidly.

Explain why the population on island R increased rapidly.  [1]
Question 34.  The diagram shows white and black moths.  The moths frequently rest on tree trunks.
The bark of tree trunks in Town A was light in colour.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Which moth would survive better in Town A?  Explain your answer.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text:
After several years, air pollution caused the bark of tree trunks in Town A to turn dark in colour.

Based on this information, what would happen to the population size of both moths in Town A after several years?  Explain your answer.  [2]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: The life cycle of the moth is similar to that of the butterfly.  The graph shows the mass of a moth during different stages of its life cycle.

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: Name stage C of the life cycle of the moth.  Suggest a reason why there is no gain in mass during stage C.  [1]
Question 35.  Samy conducted an experiment using the set-ups below.  He placed four similar test tubes A, B, C and D in different corners of a dim room.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: The graph shows the amount of water in the test tubes over a period of time.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Based on the graph,

elements.label: (a)(i)
elements.text: explain why different distances of light from the plant affect the amount of water lost.  [1]

elements.label: (a)(ii)
elements.text: which variable, light or wind, has a greater effect on the amount of water lost?  Give a reason for your answer.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Samy observed that a plant P growing in a sunny area has smaller leaves than the same type of plant growing in a shaded area.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Based on Samy's experiment, suggest a reason why leaf A is smaller in size than leaf B.  [1]

Question 36.  A greenhouse provides a suitable environment for growing certain crops.

A farmer planted some strawberries in his greenhouse.  To produce sweet strawberries, he must ensure that the plants get at least 8 hours of sunlight daily.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Suggest how having at least 8 hours of sunlight daily may help in producing sweet strawberries.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text:
In some cold countries, greenhouses are built next to a power station.  There are pipes to transport the waste gases and heat produced by the power station to the greenhouses.

Suggest one benefit of this arrangement to the environment.  [1]
Question 37.  Liwen conducted an experiment using four identical tubes as shown.
After a few hours, a drop of liquid Y was added to each tube.  When liquid Y was added, the colour of water changed according to the amount of carbon dioxide present.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Each number below represents a different colour.  The number for the colour of the water in tube A is 4.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text:
Suggest a number for the colour of the water in tubes B and D.  [1]

Tube B: ____________   Tube D: ____________

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Liwen predicted that tube C has more carbon dioxide than tube A.  However, her friend Santhi said that this may not be true.  Explain why tube C may not have more carbon dioxide than tube A.  [2]
Question 38.  When air became very cold suddenly, cracks appeared on the pavement.
Explain how the change in temperature caused the pavement to crack.  [2]
Question 39.  Xiaoming conducted an experiment using the set-ups shown.  The two beakers were identical and contained hot water at the same temperature.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: After some time, Xiaoming found that the temperature of water in set-up B was higher than that in set-up A.  Explain why.  [2]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Animal Q below lives in cold countries.  It has a thick layer of fur on its body.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Based on the results of Xiaoming's experiment, explain how the thick layer of fur of animal Q would help to keep it warm.  [1]
Question 40.  Jiaxian used the set-up shown to investigate the magnetic force between two magnets.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Explain why magnet A stayed in the same position above magnet B as shown in Diagram 1.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Jiaxian pushed magnet A towards magnet B as shown in Diagram 2.  He found that he needed to push harder as distance P decreased.  Explain why.  [1]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text:
A toy ball, G, floated above a base because of magnetic force as shown in Diagram 3.

A second toy ball, H, had a rod through its centre that was connected to a base as shown in Diagram 4.  Both balls were made of the same material and of the same size.

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: Jiaxian rotated both balls with the same amount of force.  Explain why H stopped rotating around the rod after a few rounds but G continued to rotate many more rounds before stopping.  [1]
Question 41.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text:
Huiting's mother bought a new pot that has a layer of material that prevents food from sticking to its surface during cooking.

Huiting's mother stopped her when she wanted to use a metal scrub to clean the pot.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Explain how the metal scrub will damage the pot.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Huiting wanted to find out the hardness of two materials H and F.  She used a rounded object made of material G and forced it into each material with the same force.  The results of her experiment are shown below.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: List materials F, G, H in increasing order of hardness.  Explain your answer.  [2]
Question 42.  Haziq used the set-up shown to find out how the type of bulb affects the temperature of its surrounding.

Bulb R was lit up.  He covered the box and recorded the temperature of the air in the box over a period of 10 minutes.  Then he removed bulb R and left the box uncovered for 15 minutes before repeating the experiment with bulb S and then bulb T.  The results are shown in the table.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: Fill in the boxes to show the energy changes in the bulb.  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: Give a reason why Haziq left the box uncovered for 15 minutes before repeating the experiment with the next bulb.  [1]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: Which bulb, R, S or T should Haziq use to light up his room so as to reduce the amount of energy used by the air conditioner?  Give a reason for your answer.  [1]
Question 43.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: An inflated balloon was placed in a toy rocket as shown below.  When air was released from the balloon, the toy rocket moved upwards.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: After moving some distance, no more air was released from the balloon at G.  Explain why the toy rocket was still able to move from point G to the highest point H and then fell downwards.  [2]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: The diagram below shows the path of a balloon moving in many different directions when air escapes from it.

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: If there is no wind in the room, suggest a reason why the balloon does not move in a straight line.  [1]

Question 44.  A ride at an amusement park for children becomes unsafe when the mass of a child is too high.  Therefore, a child can board the ride only if his/her mass is less than 40 kg.

Set-up A is used to detect a child with mass of 40 kg or more.

elements.label: (a)
elements.text: What type of material are the contacts made of?  Why?  [1]

elements.label: (b)
elements.text: An engineer says that the design of set-up A is not safe.  He says it will be safer if the bulb lights up when a child of acceptable mass stands on it.  Give a reason why the design of set-up A is not safe.  [1]

elements.label: (c)
elements.text: How should set-up A be changed to allow a child who is 50 kg and below to board the ride?  [1]
